title: Editor
content:
We are looking for an experienced editor to join our award-winning team at Balkan Insight, BIRN’s flagship English-language publication, and help shape the future of independent journalism in the Balkans.

Balkan Insight is the leading English-language news and investigative website covering Southeast Europe, produced by the largest independent media network in the region.

The selected candidate will play a central role in managing editorial production and maintaining the highest possible journalistic standards.

See vacancy
snippet: We are looking for an experienced editor to join our award-winning team at Balkan Insight, BIRN’s flagship English-language publication, and help shape the future of independent journalism in the Balk
creator: Emily O'Sullivan

title: Senior Editor, Research
content:
We are seeking a senior editor to lead ProPublica’s growing research team, guiding high-impact investigative work across the newsroom while supporting rigorous, collaborative and responsive research at scale.

The research team plays a critical role in many of ProPublica’s most impactful and widely read stories, contributing deep reporting, verification and sourcing to both long-term investigations and fast-turn accountability journalism. Research reporters regularly earn co-bylines and contributor credits across text, audio and video projects, and they collaborate closely with reporters, editors and partner newsrooms across the country.

Researchers typically balance two to three long-term projects while fielding short-term needs, providing expertise in public records, financial and government research, open-source intelligence and advanced verification.

This role reports to the assistant managing editor, national. You’ll lead ProPublica’s seven-member research team that works collaboratively across our national, local, Texas and Local Reporting Network desks.
snippet: We are seeking a senior editor to lead ProPublica’s growing research team, guiding high-impact investigative work across the newsroom while supporting rigorous, collaborative and responsive research a
creator: Emily O'Sullivan

title: Engagement Reporter
content:
Engagement reporters do ProPublica’s signature crowdsourced investigations, using everything from callouts and tip lines to citizen-fueled science. As an engagement reporter, you’ll team up with colleagues across the country to gather evidence and receipts for hard-hitting, community-driven stories. You may also work with external partners on long- and short-term projects through our Local Reporting Network. You’ll find and reach people who have important stories to tell, figure out how to include them in the reporting process and work on journalism that could help improve their lives. You’ll likely come across more stories and new leads in the process.

Like everyone in our newsroom, our team focuses on accountability journalism and measures success by impact. We’ve worked with communities to tell thousands of people’s stories, including educators, parents, mental health providers, wildfire survivors, migrant dairy farm workers, vulnerable workers and residents living near toxic hot spots. We’ve filled information gaps with calculators, guides and letter generators. And we’ve done our best to reach people in the spaces where they gather, both online and off. This journali
snippet: Engagement reporters do ProPublica’s signature crowdsourced investigations, using everything from callouts and tip lines to citizen-fueled science. As an engagement reporter, you’ll team up with colle
creator: Emily O'Sullivan

title: Deputy Director
content:
Index is an independent investigative NGO founded in 2020, specializing in open-source intelligence (OSINT), audiovisual analysis, and 3D reconstruction. Its work focuses primarily on police violence and human rights violations in France. A member of the Investigative Commons network and affiliated with Forensic Architecture, Index has covered major cases of police violence over the past decade and produced investigations recognized for their methodological rigor.

The Deputy Director is responsible for the operational management of Index. This is a cross-functional role with a strong focus on development. The successful candidate will manage the day-to-day operations of the organization and become the primary driver of its resource growth.

This recruitment is strategic: it will allow the Managing Director to refocus on editorial direction, survey production, and research, by entrusting operational management, resource development, and communication to a trusted individual.

See vacancy
snippet: Index is an independent investigative NGO founded in 2020, specializing in open-source intelligence (OSINT), audiovisual analysis, and 3D reconstruction. Its work focuses primarily on police violence
creator: Emily O'Sullivan

title: Investigative Journalist
content:
ICIJ is looking for a seasoned investigative reporter to join the team. This role will be responsible for researching, writing, and editing independent investigative stories that hold the powerful to account and that uncover broken systems, incisively and in the public interest. It is an opportunity to independently investigate some of the most important topics of our time, to tell stories that bring startling truths to light, to optimize analysis and coverage, and to do so with a fiercely committed, award-winning team.

ICIJ is a nonprofit online media organization with headquarters in Washington, D.C.  The core of ICIJ’s work is the coordination of major journalism investigations that cover stories that cross borders, using the latest technology to facilitate those collaborations. ICIJ is an award-winning organization including a Pulitzer Prize and a Nobel Prize nomination. ICIJ is dedicated to building a culturally diverse and pluralistic staff committed to teaching and working in a multicultural environment.

See vacancy
snippet: ICIJ is looking for a seasoned investigative reporter to join the team. This role will be responsible for researching, writing, and editing independent investigative stories that hold the powerful to
creator: Emily O'Sullivan

title: Hungary’s Historic Election, Artemis II’s Lunar Journey, Strait of Hormuz Shipping Crisis, and Three Years of War in Sudan
content: After completing NASA’s first crewed fly-by of the Moon in more than 50 years, the Artemis II team returned to Earth — drawing worldwide fascination, particularly from Australia’s ABC, which took readers on a visual journey through the mission and the space milestones that preceded it. Our roundup of the Top 10 in Data Journalism considers stories from March 30 to April 15, and also features Atlatszo’s live-tracking of the seismic Hungarian election that ended Prime Minister Viktor Orbán’s 16-year rule, the South China Morning Post’s reconstruction of a deadly Hong Kong housing fire, the Guardian’s look at how Earth’s orbit is becoming crowded with satellites, and Sky News’s assessment of Russian dominance in the Arctic.
snippet: After completing NASA’s first crewed fly-by of the Moon in more than 50 years, the Artemis II team returned to Earth — drawing worldwide fascination, particularly from Australia’s ABC, which took read
creator: Hanna Duggal

title: The African Investigative Journalist of the Year on Deep Dives, Funding, and ‘Paying Your Dues’
content:
Dewald van Rensburg uses forensic skills built over 20 years of reporting on the South African economy to piece together small details into big picture investigative stories. He has built a reputation for his deep dives into the country’s money laundering industry, dodgy bank deals, and corruption, and a few months ago was named African Investigative Journalist of the Year.

“Sometimes you have to take seriously the cliché about journalism being the first draft of history.” — Dewald van Rensburg

But his path to the limelight involved serious commitment and hard work, or as he puts it, more than a decade spent “in the wilderness,” slugging through paperwork and spreadsheets to find stories.

His breakthrough came in 2018, after uncorking the lead on the collapse of VBS Mutual Bank, a once little-known institution that gai
snippet: Dewald van Rensburg uses forensic skills built over 20 years of reporting on the South African economy to piece together small details into big picture investigative stories. He has built a reputation
creator: Banjo Damilola

title: Head of Investigations
content:
The Head of Investigations role is an exciting opportunity for an experienced investigative journalist to get involved in a new, impact-focused project from the very beginning. The project will bring together investigative journalism and advocacy work focused on issues related to chemical use in the UK. It will focus on chemical harms to both human health and environment and dig deeper into the influence of the chemicals industry.

Working closely with the project’s Founder in a team of two, the Head of Investigations is a varied role which includes designing, conducting and publishing investigations on issues related to UK chemicals. The successful candidate will also co-lead multi-disciplinary project teams comprised of investigative journalists and NGO policy experts.

It’s a unique chance for an investigative journalist that’s passionate about preventing harms to health and environment to see their work have real-world impact.

Pesticide Action Network UK (PAN UK) is an independent, non-profit organisation, which works nationally and internationally to eliminate the use of the most hazardous pesticides and promote sustainable alternatives. The organisation
snippet: The Head of Investigations role is an exciting opportunity for an experienced investigative journalist to get involved in a new, impact-focused project from the very beginning. The project will bring
creator: Emily O'Sullivan

title: Junior Content Producer, Digital (India)
content:
The BBC Media Action office in India is looking for a passionate storyteller based out of Bengaluru who understands how social media and content uniquely drive information and action in today’s digital environment. The right candidate will support the end-to-end production of digital content for social media including pre-production, filming, editing, and postproduction. They will also support other projects using their social media skills, creating and producing social media and other digital content across platforms. The role reports into the Executive Creative Director and will be responsible for producing weekly outputs of the highest creative and editorial standard for BBC Media Action social media channels.

See vacancy
snippet: The BBC Media Action office in India is looking for a passionate storyteller based out of Bengaluru who understands how social media and content uniquely drive information and action in today’s digita
creator: Emily O'Sullivan

title: How to Verify Bystander Video
content:
Bystander and surveillance videos keep driving major news stories.

The Star Tribune’s work on the Good and Pretti shootings is a case in point. A team of visual journalists deconstructed bystander footage frame by frame, directly contradicting claims made by members of the Trump administration about both killings.

The killings of Renee Good and Alex Pretti by US federal officers in Minneapolis. Brutal takedowns of lawful citizens and aggressive clashes by Immigration and Customs Enforcement across the US. The
snippet: Bystander and surveillance videos keep driving major news stories. The Star Tribune’s work on the Good and Pretti shootings is a case in point. A team of visual journalists deconstructed bystander foo
creator: Alex Mahadevan, Poynter

title: GIJN Launches Global Academy of Investigative Journalism
content:
Today, we are launching our global academy of investigative journalism, GIJN Academy, a unique hub dedicated to connect, expand, and support the journalism community around the world through key training, networking, and knowledge-sharing opportunities. This initiative is an important next step for the organization that brings together a space for new generations of reporters, practicing investigative journalists, as well as academics, journalism trainers, and experts in relevant areas.

At a time of major challenges around the world, including democracy under threat, the climate crisis, exiled media, rampant online disinformation, and more, GIJN Academy aims to grow our efforts focused on building the infrastructure that supports and helps develop resilience among investigative journalists by sharing the skills, resources, and connections needed to go after abuses of power and accountability with state-of-the-art tools, techniques,
snippet: Today, we are launching our global academy of investigative journalism, GIJN Academy, a unique hub dedicated to connect, expand, and support the journalism community around the world through key train
creator: GIJN Staff

title: Digital Threats in the Age of AI: Free Cyber Investigations Training Course in Fall 2026
content:
Digital threats are everywhere, reshaping how we live, work, and communicate. Disinformation, trolling, malware, spyware, and other forms of digital manipulation are becoming increasingly sophisticated, and AI is fueling the whole landscape. For investigative reporters, developing an AI-literate mindset has never been more critical.

In this training course, reporters from around the world will learn how to identify and investigate digital threats in the current AI-infused digital environment — and how to use AI to assist with exposing attacks and manipulation. The goal is to provide them with the expertise needed to deliver cyber investigations across a range of beats and topics.

For the fifth time, GIJN is working with renowned journalist Craig Silverman and a group of cutting-edge trainers to offer a unique cyber investigations training program specifically tailored for investigati
snippet: Digital threats are everywhere, reshaping how we live, work, and communicate. Disinformation, trolling, malware, spyware, and other forms of digital manipulation are becoming increasingly sophisticate
creator: GIJN Staff

title: Data Collecting for Journalists — Practical Training
content:
Note: On-demand courses are fee-based, allowing us to sustainably support our team and trainers while continuing to provide free, high-quality investigative journalism training opportunities worldwide.

In today’s journalism, data has become one of the most critical elements of reporting. Going beyond traditional news sources and gathering information from publicly available data, websites, or digital documents is no longer just a nice-to-have skill, it is an essential competency for the profession. This training aims to teach journalists the data collection process using practical tools and hands-on techniques.

This program, built around collecting both qualitative and quantitative data, digital research, and information extraction, will enable participants to strengthen their investigations and diversify their sources. Each module is designed with contemporary examples and prioritizes applied
snippet: Note: On-demand courses are fee-based, allowing us to sustainably support our team and trainers while continuing to provide free, high-quality investigative journalism training opportunities worldwide
creator: GIJN Staff

title: AI Without the Guesswork: Smarter Journalism Without Prompting and Hallucinations
content:
Note: On-demand courses are fee-based, allowing us to sustainably support our team and trainers while continuing to provide free, high-quality investigative journalism training opportunities worldwide.

A six-hour online masterclass for investigative journalists with Henk van Ess.

There is a built-in flaw affecting every AI conversation a journalist has and almost nobody is addressing it:  writing an AI prompt does not involve  asking it a neutral question. Instead, you are priming the tool  to answer a certain way — through your word choice, your framing, and what you leave out. The tool takes in that request and returns it to you, tidied. You read the reply and feel informed. What actually happened is that you had a conversation with a cleaner, faster version of your own assumptions.

In this course, van Ess will explain why you should never ask a bot to “summarize this,” “give me bullet points,” or “what is the main point?”

We start with
snippet: Note: On-demand courses are fee-based, allowing us to sustainably support our team and trainers while continuing to provide free, high-quality investigative journalism training opportunities worldwide
creator: GIJN Staff

title: Tips and Tools for Investigating Leaks
content:
In this masterclass video, Frederik Obermaier, co-founder of Paper Trail Media and one of the journalists behind the Panama Papers, shares practical advice on how to investigate leaks.

He explains how journalists can find and work with leaks today, from making themselves visible to whistleblowers to identifying relevant datasets and platforms where leaks are already available. Obermaier highlights key tools such as Aleph and Datashare, and shows how to search, organize, and analyze large volumes of data efficiently.

He also walks through essential steps (from verifying documents and asking the right questions to spotting stories within massive datasets) showing how leak investigations have evolved since the Panama Papers and why the real challenge today is turning data into impactful stories.

Additional resources / tools mentioned in this video:

  • OCCRP Aleph:
snippet: In this masterclass video, Frederik Obermaier, co-founder of Paper Trail Media and one of the journalists behind the Panama Papers, shares practical advice on how to investigate leaks. He explains how
creator: Rectangle

title: Inside Cross-Border Investigations
content:
In this masterclass video, Maria Teresa Ronderos, director of the Latin American Center for Investigative Journalism (El CLIP), shares practical insights on how to coordinate cross-border investigations.

Drawing on years of experience managing collaborative projects, she explains why patience and strong communication are essential when working across countries, cultures, and newsroom realities. Ronderos outlines key strategies, from selecting the right partners and aligning editorial processes to pooling resources and ensuring no newsroom is left behind.

She also highlights the importance of secure communication, shared tools, and building trust within teams, showing how successful collaborations rely as much on human relationships as on reporting skills.

Credits:

  • Interview: Sarah Ulrich
  • Video Editing: Quentin Egloff
  • Music: Lionel Marçal
  • Filming: Ron Lopez, Reynald Ramirez, Hashim Hakeem
  • Supervision and Coordination: Sandrine
snippet: In this masterclass video, Maria Teresa Ronderos, director of the Latin American Center for Investigative Journalism (El CLIP), shares practical insights on how to coordinate cross-border investigatio
creator: Rectangle

title: How to Investigate Online Scams
content:
In this masterclass video, Damien Leloup, investigative journalist at Le Monde, gives detailed instructions on how to find and investigate online scams.

Based on his own reporting, Leloup explains what journalists can do to identify an online scam, which platforms they can use, and the best tools to dig deep into the world of online scammers. He also gives important advice on how to investigate ad libraries, how to keep track of and organize your data, and why it is important to reach out to victims of scams to get a full picture of the impact of online scams in your coverage.

Credits:

  • Interview: Sandrine Rigaud
  • Video Editing: Quentin Egloff
  • Filming: Grégoire Lenoir
  • Music: Lionel Marçal
snippet: In this masterclass video, Damien Leloup, investigative journalist at Le Monde, gives detailed instructions on how to find and investigate online scams. Based on his own reporting, Leloup explains wha
creator: Rectangle

title: Producing Investigative Podcasts
content:
In this masterclass video, investigative reporter and audio journalism producer Susanne Reber shares key tips on how to investigate, write, and produce a compelling investigative podcast.

Reber guides you through the most important steps in producing a compelling audio story. She also shares insights on how you get the humanity out of a moment that makes for a good audio piece, how you encourage interviewees to a more visual style of storytelling, and gives advice on what details journalists should pay attention to: “If it’s not on tape, it’s not gonna make the show!”

  • Interview: Sarah Ulrich
  • Video Editing: Quentin Egloff
  • Music: Lionel Marçal
  • Filming: Ron Lopez, Reynald Ramirez, Hashim Hakeem
  • Supervision and Coordination: Sandrine Rigaud
snippet: In this masterclass video, investigative reporter and audio journalism producer Susanne Reber shares key tips on how to investigate, write, and produce a compelling investigative podcast. Reber guides
creator: Rectangle

title: How to Succeed as a Freelance Investigative Journalist
content:
In this masterclass video, Japan-based freelance journalist Nithin Coca shares practical lessons from 10 years of independent reporting across Asia. Drawing on his experience, he offers essential tips for building a sustainable freelance career in investigative journalism.

Freelance journalism is not only reporting, but also running a business. Here, freelancer Nithin Coca shows why you must invest in your skills and reputation, stay highly organized, and network constantly to find strong collaborators. He explains how to nail your grant applications and secure editor support, while insisting that grants don’t replace newsroom payments.

He urges reporters to collaborate to go further: connect across borders, combine knowledge, and use teamwork to turn local investigations into impactful regional stories.

Credits:

  • Interview: Sarah Ulrich
  • Video Editing: Quentin Egloff
  • Music: Lionel Marçal
  • Filming: Ron Lopez, Reynald Ramirez, Hashim Hakeem
snippet: In this masterclass video, Japan-based freelance journalist Nithin Coca shares practical lessons from 10 years of independent reporting across Asia. Drawing on his experience, he offers essential tips
creator: Rectangle

title: Investigating AI-Generated and Manipulated Images
content:
In this masterclass video, Craig Silverman, investigative journalist and co-founder of Indicator, shares practical techniques for analyzing and verifying images in an era of misinformation and AI-generated content.

He explains why identifying the origin of an image — iits source, location, and timing — is often the most critical step in any verification process. Silverman walks through key methods, from close visual analysis and reverse image searches to checking metadata and using specialized tools.

  • Interview: Sarah Ulrich
  • Video Editing: Quentin Egloff
  • Music: Lionel Marçal
  • Filming: Ron Lopez, Reynald Ramirez, Hashim Hakeem
  • Supervision and Coordination: Sandrine Rigaud
